zotero.abstractNote: Curriculum learning is a powerful paradigm for training neural networks, yet designing effective curricula remains a significant challenge. We propose Modular Growth of Hierarchical Networks (MGHN), a novel framework that automates curriculum design by growing network architectures in tandem with task complexity. Inspired by the modular and hierarchical organization of biological brains, MGHN starts with a small, simple network and incrementally adds modules to solve increasingly complex tasks. We demonstrate that MGHN is efficient, general, and robust across a variety of reinforcement learning and supervised learning tasks. Our results show that MGHN outperforms standard curriculum learning methods and static architectures, providing a scalable approach to building complex, hierarchical representations.
